neighborhoods.name: Bastide Saint-Louis (Carcassonne)
neighborhoods.description: The lower town grid of Carcassonne with tree-lined squares, markets, and shops below the medieval citadel
neighborhoods.character: Historic grid town, market culture, citadel views

housing.predominantType: Apartments in 18th-century townhouses and newer buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Montpellier. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Narbonne
neighborhoods.description: Roman-founded coastal city with a vibrant covered market, canal heritage, and beach access
neighborhoods.character: Roman heritage, coastal market town, Mediterranean
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Montpellier's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Limoux
neighborhoods.description: Small town south of Carcassonne famous for sparkling wine and carnival, with affordable housing
neighborhoods.character: Small wine town, carnival traditions, affordable
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Beziers / Canal du Midi Area
neighborhoods.description: Revitalizing city on the Canal du Midi with affordable property, growing expat scene, and wine culture

housing.predominantType: Renovated apartments, townhouses, and canal-side properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
